Both files are written and validated. Everything checks out.

`‚òÖ Insight ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ`
- **The validation caught nothing broken ‚Äî which is the point.** All dates match `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`, categories collapsed to exactly 5 clean title-case names, regions to 5 (including `Unknown`), and no row has an empty units/price. Verifying with `grep -v`/`awk` on the *output* is stronger than trusting the script logic.
- **The dedup/drop accounting is layered:** 285 raw rows ‚Üí 5 exact duplicates removed ‚Üí 280 unique ‚Üí 158 dropped for missing units/price ‚Üí **122 kept**. I reported kept/dropped against the deduped set, treating duplicate removal as a distinct upstream step.
`‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ`

## Results

**`cleaned_sales.csv`** ‚Äî 122 clean rows. Duplicates removed (5 exact dupes), all dates normalized to `YYYY-MM-DD` (from four different input formats), categories title-cased/trimmed to 5 canonical names, regions title-cased with blanks ‚Üí `Unknown`, and every row missing `units_sold` or `unit_price` dropped.

**`summary.md`** ‚Äî the full analysis:

- **Rows kept:** 122  ‚Ä¢  **Rows dropped:** 158 (missing units or price)
- **Total revenue:** **$347,862.18**
- **Revenue by category:** Electronics ($122.5K) ‚Ä∫ Clothing ($86.5K) ‚Ä∫ Toys ($68.1K) ‚Ä∫ Home Goods ($44.3K) ‚Ä∫ Garden ($26.5K)
- **Revenue by month:** climbs from Jan ($41.4K) to a peak in May ($76.9K), then eases in June ($67.8K)

One judgment call worth flagging: **158 of 280 unique rows (56%) were dropped** for missing numeric fields ‚Äî a lot of the export was incomplete, so the revenue figures reflect only the ~44% of orders with complete data. If those blanks should instead be imputed (e.g., filled with category-average price) rather than dropped, that's a quick change to the script.
